mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or signal accuracy drops below 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX direction in >60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or regime-specific IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 8.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.5%, or threshold signal accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market tightness that supports corporate earnings and drives positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal predicts positive 60-day SPX direction in <55% of cases, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >65% of quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals acute supply-side stress that triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of months when INDPRO drops >1.0%, or threshold signal accuracy drops below 53% in walk-forward validation.
